results.title: Frog Detective 2: The Case of the Invisible Wizard
results.brief_description: Another mystery is afoot in Frog Detective 2: The Case of the Invisible Wizard. In the town of Warlock Woods, a parade welcoming the Invisible Wizard has been mysteriously destroyed. As the ‘second-best detective’, players interview a new cast of low-poly characters and follow clues through gentle, comic conversation.

constellations_primary.name: Soft-boiled detectives
constellations_primary.description: This Constellation follows cosy mysteries where investigation is playful, social and a little strange. In these works, detectives question odd suspects, navigate anxious communities and solve cases where charm, humour and character matter just as much as the truth itself.

results.title_for_label: Frog Detective 2: The Case of the Invisible Wizard
results.description:
Another mystery is afoot in Frog Detective 2: The Case of the Invisible Wizard. In the town of Warlock Woods, a parade welcoming the Invisible Wizard has been mysteriously destroyed. As the ‘second-best detective’, players interview a new cast of low-poly characters and follow clues through gentle, comic conversation.

This second instalment expands the series’ play style with a detective notebook, letting players track interviews and mark residents as suspicious. Composer Dan Golding also develops the game’s playful jazz score, adding different instruments as Frog Detective meets each character. The result is a mystery shaped by humour, rhythm and care.

This second instalment expands the series’ play style with a detective notebook, letting players track interviews and mark residents as suspicious. Composer Dan Golding also develops the game’s playful jazz score, adding different instruments as Frog Detective meets each character. The result is a mystery shaped by humour, rhythm and care.

results.title: Gumboots Australia for DOS
results.material_description: Floppy disk videogame. One 3 1/2 inch floppy disk and manual in cardboard box
results.brief_description: Explore Australia and help solve a plethora of mysteries in Gumboots Australia. In this this adventure and educational game, the player is tasked with solving cases as an investigator for the Gumboots Enquiry Agency, a job that will take the player all over the country. The player will get to visit locations as varied as Kakadu to Coober Pedy in their hunt for the next clue. Getting there is its own challenge, as different modes of transportation can cost time and money. Random events along the way aid or hinder the player’s quest to solve the case.

results.title: Frog Detective 3: Corruption at Cowboy County
results.brief_description:
The third and final game in the Frog Detective trilogy finds mystery in Cowboy County, where Frog Detective joins the ‘number-one detective’, Lobster Cop, to solve the case of some stolen hats.
Worm Club combines the series’ usual detective work with the sights and sounds of the Western, as players interview and help a new cast of absurd townsfolk and outlaws. Cowboy County is bigger than the earlier games’ settings, with “vast and rough” terrain to cross. What began as a gag became one of the game’s key features: a scooter system developed by Tom Bowker, turning movement itself into part of the joke.

constellations_primary.name: Classical, comedies and cowboys
constellations_primary.description: This Constellation follows the use of classical music through videogame, TV shows and cinema – often with cowboys.

results.title: Frog Detective
results.creator_credit: Grace Lindsay Bruxner, Tom Bowker and Worm Club
results.brief_description: Frog Detective is a trilogy of videogames created by Worm Club. Each of the three games represent different cases for the Frog Detective to solve. A bundled version was also released, including all three cases and an additional bonus game - Frog Detective Scooter.

results.title: Frog Detective 1: The Haunted Island
results.brief_description:
In Frog Detective 1: The Haunted Island, the stereotype of a hard-boiled detective becomes a friendly frog investigating a ghostly noise. Developed by Grace Bruxner while she was still studying game design, this first game in the Frog Detective trilogy turns detective fiction into a silly, kind-hearted adventure.
It borrows from screen mysteries like Agatha Christie’s Poirot (1989), using familiar music, camera angles and episodic structure. But the mystery is deliberately low-stakes, and the detective is polite, enthusiastic and apologetic when suspicious. Through comic dialogue and absurd interviews, Bruxner finds humour in making noir softer, stranger and much more charming.

constellations_other.name: Animal hijinks
constellations_other.description: As long as stories have been told on screen, audiences have loved films, TV shows, memes and videogames about animals being naughty. This constellation shares some key examples of animal comedy throughout the ages.
